title: ucsd-cmi/compass pipeline parameters
description: COMPASS - COmprehensive Metagenomics Platform for Automated Sequence Solutions

input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

input.description: Path to comma-separated file containing information about the samples in the experiment.
input.help_text: You will need to create a design file with information about the samples in your experiment before running the pipeline. Use this parameter to specify its location. It has to be a comma-separated file with 3 columns, and a header row.

outdir.description: The output directory where the results will be saved. You have to use absolute paths to storage on Cloud infrastructure.

email.help_text: Set this parameter to your e-mail address to get a summary e-mail with details of the run sent to you when the workflow exits. If set in your user config file (`~/.nextflow/config`) then you don't need to specify this on the command line for every run.

multiqc_title.description: MultiQC report title. Printed as page header, used for filename if not otherwise specified.

ref_databases.description: Path to comma-separated file containing information about reference databases to use in the pipeline.

workflow_mode.help_text: Determines whether the pipeline is in shotgun metagenomics mode or 16S mode.

shotgun_adapter_list.description: Path to a list of adapters to use in shotgun metagenomic sample pre-processing

shotgun_do_preprocessing.description: Determines whether to do shotgun metagenomic sample pre-processing steps
shotgun_do_preprocessing.help_text: It's generally recommended to do pre-processing unless you know it has already been performed using external tools.

shotgun_preprocessing_min_read_length.description: Minimum length of reads to preserve and process for shotgun metagenomic samples
shotgun_preprocessing_min_read_length.help_text: Changing the minimum read length will impact both profiling speed and accuracy.

shotgun_preprocessing_fastp_qualified_qu.description: Minimum phred quality score to consider a read qualified for shotgun metagenomic samples
shotgun_preprocessing_fastp_qualified_qu.help_text: Increasing this value will enact stricter quality filtering.

shotgun_preprocessing_fastp_cut_tail.description: Determines whether to use fastp's --cut_tail parameter for shotgun metagenomic samples
shotgun_preprocessing_fastp_cut_tail.help_text: --cut_tail performs sliding window trimming from the 3' (tail) end of a read, removing low-quality bases based on mean quality.

shotgun_do_host_removal.description: Determines whether to do host removal steps for shotgun metagenomic samples
shotgun_do_host_removal.help_text: It's generally recommended to perform host removal if you're interested in microbial composition

shotgun_host_removal_bowtie2_very_sensit.description: Determines whether to use the --very-sensitive parameter for Bowtie2
shotgun_host_removal_bowtie2_very_sensit.help_text: Setting this to true will help maximize host removal.

shotgun_do_read_pairing.description: Determines whether to ensure all reads in paired-end files are correctly paired for shotgun metagenomic samples
shotgun_do_read_pairing.help_text: Setting this to true will drop singletons before proceeding with taxonomic profiling.

shotgun_do_run_merging.description: Determines whether to merge shotgun metagenomic samples with the same sample_id
shotgun_do_run_merging.help_text: Optional step to merge samples prior to the profiling step.

shotgun_do_kraken2.description: Determines whether to run Kraken2 for shotgun metagenomic samples
shotgun_do_kraken2.help_text: Given that Kraken2 is currently the only profiler available, this should not be changed to false.

shotgun_do_kraken2_filtering.description: Determines whether to use KrakenTools/extract_kraken_reads to filter Kraken2 output
shotgun_do_kraken2_filtering.help_text: This allows you to either include or exclude reads with specific taxonomic IDs from downstream output. Running this will also cause Kraken2 to be re-run on this tool's output.

shotgun_kraken2_filtering_taxids.description: Space-delimited list of taxonomic IDs to filter
shotgun_kraken2_filtering_taxids.help_text: IDs can be from any taxonomic rank

shotgun_kraken2_filtering_mode.description: Determines whether the specified taxonomic IDs are included (kept) or excluded (discarded)
shotgun_kraken2_filtering_mode.help_text: Default behavior is to exclude for the purpose of ensuring host reads are fully removed.

shotgun_kraken2_filtering_include_parent.description: Determines whether parent taxonomic IDs should be included in filter
shotgun_kraken2_filtering_include_parent.help_text: Determines whether parent taxonomic IDs should be included in filter

shotgun_kraken2_filtering_include_childr.description: Determines whether child taxonomic IDs should be included in filter
shotgun_kraken2_filtering_include_childr.help_text: Determines whether child taxonomic IDs should be included in filter

shotgun_do_bracken.description: Determines whether to run Bracken for shotgun metagenomic samples
shotgun_do_bracken.help_text: Optional tool to estimate abundance of taxa within a sample. Requires Kraken2.

shotgun_profiling_standardisation_option.title: Profiling standardisation options
shotgun_profiling_standardisation_option.description: Options related to taxonomic profile standardisation using Taxpasta

shotgun_do_profiling_standardisation.description: Determines whether to run Taxpasta for shotgun metagenomic samples
shotgun_do_profiling_standardisation.help_text: Optional tool to standardise output from various taxonomic profiling tools.

shotgun_taxpasta_taxonomy.description: Path to directory containing taxdump files
shotgun_taxpasta_taxonomy.help_text: Path to directory containing taxdump files

shotgun_taxpasta_add_name.description: Add taxon name to Taxpasta output
shotgun_taxpasta_add_name.help_text: Add taxon name to Taxpasta output

shotgun_taxpasta_add_rank.description: Add taxon rank to Taxpasta output
shotgun_taxpasta_add_rank.help_text: Add taxon rank to Taxpasta output

shotgun_taxpasta_add_lineage.description: Add full taxon lineage to Taxpasta output - names

shotgun_taxpasta_add_id_lineage.description: Add full taxon lineage to Taxpasta output - IDs

shotgun_taxpasta_add_rank_lineage.description: Add full taxon rank lineage to Taxpasta output

amplicon_random_seed.description: Random number seed used for certain processing tools.
amplicon_random_seed.help_text: Can be left at the default value aside from very special circumstances.

amplicon_paired_end_data.description: Determines whether the pipeline processes incoming samples as paired-end data.
amplicon_paired_end_data.help_text: Set to true for paired-end data and false for single-end data.

amplicon_do_cutadapt.description: Determines whether cutadapt runs on incoming samples.
amplicon_do_cutadapt.help_text: Can be set to false for samples that have already been through the trimming and primer removal process.

amplicon_minimum_read_count.description: Minimum read count for a sample to be considered valid
amplicon_minimum_read_count.help_text: Minimum read count for a sample to be considered valid

amplicon_minimum_primer_overlap.description: Minimum overlap to be considered a valid match with a primer sequence.
amplicon_minimum_primer_overlap.help_text: This parameter corresponds to cutadapt's -O parameter.

amplicon_maximum_primer_error_rate.description: Maximum error rate to be considered a valid match with a primer sequence.
amplicon_maximum_primer_error_rate.help_text: This parameter corresponds to cutadapt's -e parameter

amplicon_calculate_trunclen.description: Determines whether the pipeline will automatically calculate the truncation length for read trimming and filtering.
amplicon_calculate_trunclen.help_text: If this is true, the pipeline will calculate a truncation length based on the point at which the quality score drops below the amplicon_calculate_trunclen_quality_threshold parameter. If this is false, the pipeline will use the amplicon_trunclen_forward and amplicon_trunclen_reverse parameters.

amplicon_calculate_trunclen_quality_thre.description: The minimum median quality score that will be used to calculate a truncation point for both forward and reverse reads.
amplicon_calculate_trunclen_quality_thre.help_text: The minimum median quality score that will be used to calculate a truncation point for both forward and reverse reads.

amplicon_trunclen_forward.description: The number of bases after which forward reads will be truncated, to be used only if amplicon_calculate_trunclen is false.
amplicon_trunclen_forward.help_text: If set to 0, forward reads will not be truncated.

amplicon_trunclen_reverse.description: The number of bases after which reverse reads will be truncated, to be used only if amplicon_calculate_trunclen is false.
amplicon_trunclen_reverse.help_text: If set to 0, reverse reads will not be truncated.

amplicon_truncate_below_read_quality.description: Truncate reads at the first instance of a quality score less than or equal to this value, corresponding to the truncQ parameter for DADA2's filterAndTrim function.
amplicon_truncate_below_read_quality.help_text: Truncate reads at the first instance of a quality score less than or equal to this value, corresponding to the truncQ parameter for DADA2's filterAndTrim function.

amplicon_trim_left.description: The number of nucleotides to remove from the beginning of each read during DADA2's filterAndTrim function.
amplicon_trim_left.help_text: The number of nucleotides to remove from the beginning of each read during DADA2's filterAndTrim function.

amplicon_trim_right.description: The number of nucleotides to remove from the end of each read during DADA2's filterAndTrim function.
amplicon_trim_right.help_text: The number of nucleotides to remove from the end of each read during DADA2's filterAndTrim function.

amplicon_maximum_length_before_trimming.description: Maximum read length before trimming, corresponding to the maxLen parameter for DADA2's filterAndTrim function.
amplicon_maximum_length_before_trimming.help_text: A value of -1 will disable the parameter.

amplicon_minimum_length_after_trimming.description: Minimum read length after trimming, corresponding to the minLen parametr for DADA2's filterAndTrim function.
amplicon_minimum_length_after_trimming.help_text: Minimum read length after trimming, corresponding to the minLen parametr for DADA2's filterAndTrim function.

amplicon_maximum_expected_error.description: Maximum expected errors for a read to be kept after trimming, corresponding to the maxEE parameter for DADA2's filterAndTrim function
amplicon_maximum_expected_error.help_text: A value of -1 will disable the parameter.

amplicon_abort_failed_preprocessing.description: Determines whether the pipeline will abort when any samples fall below the amplicon_minimum_read_count parameter's threshold after preprocessing.
amplicon_abort_failed_preprocessing.help_text: When this parameter is set to true, the pipeline will abort. Otherwise, the pipeline will continue with samples whose read count is greater than amplicon_minimum_read_count.

amplicon_asv_inference_options.title: ASV inference options for amplicon samples

amplicon_do_remove_chimeras.description: Determines whether chimeric reads are removed.
amplicon_do_remove_chimeras.help_text: Removal of chimeric reads should only be skipped under very specific circumstances and with caution.

amplicon_taxonomic_classification_option.title: Taxonomic classification options for amplicon samples

amplicon_do_taxonomic_classification_dad.description: Determines whether taxonomic classification via DADA2 is performed.
amplicon_do_taxonomic_classification_dad.help_text: Determines whether taxonomic classification via DADA2 is performed.

amplicon_taxonomic_classification_dada2_.description: The minimum bootstrap confidence for assigning a taxonomic level.
amplicon_taxonomic_classification_dada2_.help_text: The minimum bootstrap confidence for assigning a taxonomic level.

amplicon_do_taxonomic_classification_dad.description: Determines whether the DADA2 addSpecies function is included in taxonomic classification.
amplicon_do_taxonomic_classification_dad.help_text: Determines whether the DADA2 addSpecies function is included in taxonomic classification. Requires performing taxonomic classification via DADA2.

amplicon_taxonomic_classification_specie.description: Determines whether dada2's addSpecies function is permitted to return multiple exact species-level matches for a given sequence.
amplicon_taxonomic_classification_specie.help_text: Setting this to false will return only unique matches, while true will return a comma-delimited list of exact matches.

amplicon_do_taxonomic_classification_qii.description: Determines whether taxonomic classification via QIIME 2 is performed.
amplicon_do_taxonomic_classification_qii.help_text: Determines whether taxonomic classification via QIIME 2 is performed.

amplicon_phylogenetic_placement_options.title: Phylogenetic placement options for amplicon samples

amplicon_do_phylogenetic_placement.description: Determines if phylogenetic placement is performed.
amplicon_do_phylogenetic_placement.help_text: Determines if phylogenetic placement is performed.

amplicon_taxa_filtering_options.title: Taxa filtering options for amplicon samples

amplicon_taxa_to_exclude.description: List of taxa to exclude from downstream analysis
amplicon_taxa_to_exclude.help_text: Comma-delimited list of terms to use for taxa filtering. Terms will be both fully and partially matched, can be any level of taxa, and should match the terminology used in your selected reference database.

amplicon_minimum_abundance.description: Minimum absolute abundance to remain in the feature table
amplicon_minimum_abundance.help_text: A value of 1 will effectively disable the filter, while higher values will remove sequences with abundance counts under the threshold.

amplicon_minimum_prevalence.description: Minimum prevalence (or number of samples in which a taxon appears) to remain in the feature table
amplicon_minimum_prevalence.help_text: A value of 1 will effectively disable the filter, while higher values will remove sequences that appear in fewer samples than the threshold.

amplicon_do_rarefaction.description: Determines whether rarefaction is performed before calculating diversity indices.
amplicon_do_rarefaction.help_text: Determines whether rarefaction is performed before calculating diversity indices.

amplicon_rarefaction_minimum_sample_dept.description: Minimum sampling depth to keep samples within the set.
amplicon_rarefaction_minimum_sample_dept.help_text: Minimum sampling depth to keep samples within the set.

amplicon_taxonomy_for_analysis.description: Determines which tool's taxonomic classification to use for downstream analysis.
amplicon_taxonomy_for_analysis.help_text: Current options are qiime2 and dada2. Please ensure that you allow the taxonomic classifier of choice to run, or the pipeline will return an error. Leaving this blank will bypass all analysis steps.

amplicon_do_export_sequences_absolute.description: Determines whether an absolute abundance table of representative sequences is exported.
amplicon_do_export_sequences_absolute.help_text: Determines whether an absolute abundance table of representative sequences is exported.

amplicon_do_export_sequences_relative.description: Determines whether a relative abundance table of representative sequences is exported.
amplicon_do_export_sequences_relative.help_text: Determines whether a relative abundance table of representative sequences is exported.

amplicon_do_export_taxonomy_absolute.description: Determines whether absolute abundance tables of taxonomic features are exported.
amplicon_do_export_taxonomy_absolute.help_text: Determines whether absolute abundance tables of taxonomic features are exported.

amplicon_export_taxonomy_absolute_collap.description: Determines what taxonomic levels the absolute abundance table is collapsed to for export. Provide a comma-delimited list of integers.
amplicon_export_taxonomy_absolute_collap.help_text: Numeric values correspond to taxonomic tier from broadest (Kingdom = 1) to most specific (Species = 7). Selected levels need not be consecutive - for example a parameter of '2,4,6' would export tables collapsed to Phylum, Order, and Genus.

amplicon_do_export_taxonomy_relative.description: Determines whether relative abundance tables of taxonomic features are exported.
amplicon_do_export_taxonomy_relative.help_text: Determines whether relative abundance tables of taxonomic features are exported.

amplicon_export_taxonomy_relative_collap.description: Determines what taxonomic levels the relative abundance table is collapsed to for export. Provide a comma-delimited list of integers.
amplicon_export_taxonomy_relative_collap.help_text: Numeric values correspond to taxonomic tier from broadest (Kingdom = 1) to most specific (Species = 7). Selected levels need not be consecutive - for example a parameter of '2,4,6' would export tables collapsed to Phylum, Order, and Genus.

amplicon_do_export_taxonomy_barplot.description: Determines whether QIIME 2's built-in barplot feature is used to generate a visualization of the taxonomy.
amplicon_do_export_taxonomy_barplot.help_text: Determines whether QIIME 2's built-in barplot feature is used to generate a visualization of the taxonomy.

amplicon_do_alpha_diversity.description: Determines whether alpha diversity is calculated and returned.
amplicon_do_alpha_diversity.help_text: Determines whether alpha diversity is calculated and returned.

amplicon_alpha_diversity_metrics.description: Comma-delimited list of which metrics should be used to calculate alpha diversity.
amplicon_alpha_diversity_metrics.help_text: Please refer to https://docs.qiime2.org/2024.10/plugins/available/diversity/alpha/ for a full list of valid metrics.

amplicon_do_alpha_diversity_phylogenetic.description: Determines whether phylogenetic alpha diversity is calculated and returned.
amplicon_do_alpha_diversity_phylogenetic.help_text: Determines whether phylogenetic alpha diversity is calculated and returned.

amplicon_alpha_diversity_phylogenetic_me.description: Comma-delimited list of which metrics should be used to calculate phylogenetic alpha diversity.
amplicon_alpha_diversity_phylogenetic_me.help_text: Currently, faith_pd is the only available metric.

amplicon_do_beta_diversity.description: Determines whether beta diversity is calculated and returned.
amplicon_do_beta_diversity.help_text: Determines whether beta diversity is calculated and returned.

amplicon_beta_diversity_metrics.description: Comma-delimited list of which metrics should be used to calculate beta diversity.
amplicon_beta_diversity_metrics.help_text: Please refer to https://docs.qiime2.org/2024.10/plugins/available/diversity/beta/ for a full list of valid metrics.

amplicon_do_beta_diversity_phylogenetic.description: Determines whether phylogenetic beta diversity is calculated and returned.
amplicon_do_beta_diversity_phylogenetic.help_text: Determines whether phylogenetic beta diversity is calculated and returned.

amplicon_beta_diversity_phylogenetic_met.description: Comma-delimited list of which metrics should be used to calculate phylogenetic beta diversity.
amplicon_beta_diversity_phylogenetic_met.help_text: Please refer to https://docs.qiime2.org/2024.10/plugins/available/diversity/beta-phylogenetic/ for a full list of valid metrics.

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.
generic_options.help_text:
These options are common to all nf-core pipelines and allow you to customise some of the core preferences for how the pipeline runs.

Typically these options would be set in a Nextflow config file loaded for all pipeline runs, such as `~/.nextflow/config`.

publish_dir_mode.description: Method used to save pipeline results to output directory.
publish_dir_mode.help_text: The Nextflow `publishDir` option specifies which intermediate files should be saved to the output directory. This option tells the pipeline what method should be used to move these files. See [Nextflow docs](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/process.html#publishdir) for details.

email_on_fail.description: Email address for completion summary, only when pipeline fails.
email_on_fail.help_text: An email address to send a summary email to when the pipeline is completed - ONLY sent if the pipeline does not exit successfully.

plaintext_email.description: Send plain-text email instead of HTML.

max_multiqc_email_size.description: File size limit when attaching MultiQC reports to summary emails.

monochrome_logs.description: Do not use coloured log outputs.

multiqc_config.description: Custom config file to supply to MultiQC.

multiqc_logo.description: Custom logo file to supply to MultiQC. File name must also be set in the MultiQC config file

multiqc_methods_description.description: Custom MultiQC yaml file containing HTML including a methods description.

validate_params.description: Boolean whether to validate parameters against the schema at runtime

pipelines_testdata_base_path.description: Base URL or local path to location of pipeline test dataset files

trace_report_suffix.description: Suffix to add to the trace report filename. Default is the date and time in the format yyyy-MM-dd_HH-mm-ss.

help_full.description: Display the full detailed help message.

show_hidden.description: Display hidden parameters in the help message (only works when --help or --help_full are provided).
